summary: Fixed the /historico page 'Application error: a client-side exception has occurred' bug. Root cause: Empty string values in Select.Item components from movements with empty userId. Fixed by filtering out empty/falsy user IDs in activeUserIds useMemo.
passed_tests: /historico page loads without 'Application error' after fix
Filters section visible with all dropdowns working
Stats cards (Entradas, Saidas, Env. Reforma, etc.) visible
Registro de Movimentacoes table visible with data
Supabase data loads successfully (console shows '✅ Dados carregados do Supabase com sucesso!')
context_for_next_testing_agent: The /historico page bug was caused by empty userId values in movements data from Supabase. The fix filters out empty strings before rendering Select.Item components. All pages now load correctly.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: The activeUserIds useMemo in /app/frontend/app/(dashboard)/historico/page.tsx was collecting all userId values from movements, including empty strings. When these empty strings were used as values for Select.Item components, it caused a React error: 'A <Select.Item /> must have a value prop that is not an empty string'
reproduction_steps: Page crashes with 'Application error: a client-side exception has occurred'
Browser console shows: 'A <Select.Item /> must have a value prop that is not an empty string'
rca_of_the_issue.fix_applied: Added filter to activeUserIds useMemo: movements.map((m) => m.userId).filter((id) => id && id.trim() !== '')
